assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading the L4 inference pool and A100 pool separately with different strategies
assertions.reason: The response explicitly separates the upgrade plan into L4 (inference) and A100 (fine-tuning) pools with completely different strategies for each.

assertions.expectation: For L4 inference pool: recommends surge upgrade with maxSurge=1 or 2 to maintain capacity during rolling replacement
assertions.reason: The response recommends a surge upgrade but specifies maxSurge=5 (or maxUnavailable=5) rather than the specific values of 1 or 2.

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPU driver version compatibility — auto-installed drivers must be compatible with inference framework (TensorRT, vLLM, etc.)
assertions.reason: The response highlights that GKE auto-installs NVIDIA drivers, which may change the CUDA version and break inference framework (PyTorch/TensorFlow) compatibility.

assertions.expectation: For A100 fine-tuning pool: recommends scheduling the upgrade during a gap between fine-tuning jobs
assertions.reason: The response recommends a 'Cordon and Wait' strategy, advising to halt new jobs, wait for active jobs to complete, and upgrade once nodes are idle.

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPU VMs do not support live migration — inference pods will restart during node upgrade, causing brief latency spikes
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states that 'GPU VMs do not support live migration, every node upgrade requires a pod restart'.

assertions.expectation: Recommends PDBs on inference deployments to ensure enough replicas stay available during the rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response advises applying PodDisruptionBudgets (PDBs) to inference deployments so GKE respects minimum available replica counts.

assertions.expectation: Addresses auto-scaler interaction — new nodes created by auto-scaler during upgrade will be at the old version until their pool upgrade completes
assertions.reason: The response mentions the auto-scaler but fails to note that new nodes created by the auto-scaler during the upgrade will be at the old version until the upgrade finishes.

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing driver + CUDA compatibility in staging before production upgrade
assertions.reason: The response recommends spinning up nodes in a dev environment/sandbox to verify driver and CUDA compatibility before touching production.
